name: Edison Scientific
description: Full-stack AI scientist platform that autonomously generates hypotheses, designs experiments, analyzes data, and writes research reports. Spun out of FutureHouse with backing from Spark Capital.
approach: Multi-agent orchestration with world model for end-to-end scientific research
notes: Spun out from FutureHouse, Eric Schmidt-backed AI research nonprofit. Aims to automate the full scientific method.

name: Sakana AI
description: Japanese AI lab that published the landmark 'AI Scientist' paper demonstrating autonomous research paper generation. Raised over $520M at a $2.65B valuation.
approach: Nature-inspired AI; evolutionary and swarm-based model development
notes: Published 'The AI Scientist' (Aug 2024), first fully automated scientific paper generation system. Google strategic investment in Jan 2026.

name: Lila Sciences
description: Autonomous lab platform combining AI-driven experiment design with robotic execution. Flagship Pioneering portfolio company building closed-loop scientific discovery systems.
approach: Closed-loop AI + robotics for autonomous wet-lab experimentation
notes: Unveiled Mar 2025 with $200M seed — largest seed round for AI science startup at the time. Total $550M committed by Flagship.

name: Periodic Labs
description: Building autonomous laboratories where AI agents design, execute, and iterate on experiments without human intervention. Founded by ex-OpenAI and DeepMind researchers.
approach: AI-controlled robotic labs with foundation models for experimental science
notes: Record $300M seed round (Sep 2025). Focus on materials science and chemistry automation.

name: K-Dense (Biostate AI)
description: AI research agent platform (formerly Biostate AI) that helps scientists navigate literature, generate hypotheses, and analyze experimental data across biology and chemistry.
approach: LLM-powered research copilot with deep domain knowledge graphs
notes: Rebranded from Biostate AI to K-Dense. Focuses on knowledge-dense scientific reasoning.

name: Potato
description: AI research agent that automates literature review, data extraction, and synthesis for scientists. Focused on making research accessible and accelerating discovery.
approach: Automated research workflows with structured data extraction
notes: YC-backed. Focus on automating tedious parts of the research process.

name: Kepler AI
description: AI research agent building autonomous systems for scientific literature analysis, experimental planning, and research synthesis.
approach: Agentic AI for end-to-end research workflows
notes: Early-stage stealth company. Building AI agents for scientific research workflows.

name: Ascent Bio
description: Yale spinout applying AI to drug discovery, combining computational biology with novel target identification and lead optimization.
approach: AI-driven target identification and molecular design
notes: Academic spinout focused on AI-driven drug discovery from Yale computational biology lab.

name: Bio Protocol BIOS
description: Decentralized science (DeSci) platform using blockchain and AI agents to coordinate and fund open scientific research. BIOS is the AI agent layer for autonomous research.
approach: Blockchain-coordinated AI agents for decentralized scientific research
notes: Leading DeSci protocol. BIOS agents perform literature review and generate research reports autonomously.

name: SciSpace (Typeset)
description: AI-powered research platform for literature discovery, reading, and writing. Copilot helps researchers understand papers, extract data, and generate literature reviews.
approach: AI copilot for academic research with paper understanding and writing assistance
notes: Originally Typeset, rebranded to SciSpace. Over 5M researchers use the platform for literature review and paper writing.

name: FutureHouse
description: Nonprofit AI research organization backed by Eric Schmidt, building open-source AI tools for scientific research including PaperQA and other research automation systems.
approach: Open-source AI tools for literature search, synthesis, and scientific reasoning
notes: Parent org of Edison Scientific spinout. Built PaperQA, one of the most cited AI research tools. Eric Schmidt personally funded.

name: Recursion Pharmaceuticals
description: Public company (RXRX) combining AI, automation, and biology to industrialize drug discovery. Operates one of the largest biological datasets in the world with automated labs.
approach: High-throughput automated biology + AI models for drug discovery at scale
notes: Public (NASDAQ: RXRX). Acquired Cyclica, Valence Discovery. Acquired Exscientia for ~$688M (2024), consolidating two of the largest AI drug discovery platforms. Partnership with NVIDIA for BioNeMo. Largest AI drug discovery company by market cap.

name: Pipette.bio
description: Cloud-based AI agent for bioinformatics that enables researchers to run genomics, transcriptomics, and multi-omics analysis pipelines via natural language. Orchestrates 100+ open-source bioinformatics tools without requiring coding.
approach: Conversational AI agent orchestrating 100+ bioinformatics tools via natural language interface on cloud compute
notes: Currently in beta. Trusted by researchers at Duke, UC Berkeley, UPenn, Cleveland Clinic, NUS, UCL, and others. Integrates PubMed, ClinVar, gnomAD, UniProt, AlphaFold, and more.

name: EvolutionaryScale
description: Builds biological foundation models (ESM-3) that generate novel functional proteins from scratch. ESM-3 reasons over protein sequence, structure, and function simultaneously, enabling de novo protein design.
approach: Large-scale protein language models trained on billions of years of evolutionary data; multimodal generative AI for sequence, structure, and function
notes: ESM-3 generated a novel functional fluorescent protein — a feat evolution took 500M years. Ex-Meta FAIR team (creators of ESM protein language model family).

name: Profluent Bio
description: Generative AI for protein and gene editor design. Created OpenCRISPR-1, the world's first AI-generated gene editor validated in human cells — a biological tool designed entirely by AI that never existed in nature.
approach: Protein language models and generative diffusion models for de novo biological molecule design
notes: OpenCRISPR-1 (2024) was a landmark — first open-source AI-designed gene editor. Demonstrates AI can autonomously invent new biological tools. Ex-Salesforce AI Research.

name: Isomorphic Labs
description: Alphabet's dedicated AI drug discovery company, extending AlphaFold into end-to-end drug design. Uses deep learning for protein structure prediction, molecular interaction modeling, and generative drug design.
approach: AlphaFold-derived deep learning for protein structure, molecular dynamics simulation, and generative chemistry
notes: Pharma deals worth up to $2.9B total: $1.7B with Eli Lilly and $1.2B with Novartis (Jan 2024). AlphaFold3 is the underlying technology. Entirely Alphabet-funded.

name: Insilico Medicine
description: End-to-end AI drug discovery platform with PandaOmics (target discovery), Chemistry42 (molecule generation), and InClinico (clinical trial prediction). First company to advance a fully AI-discovered drug to Phase II clinical trials.
approach: GANs, reinforcement learning, and transformers for target identification, de novo molecule generation, and clinical outcome prediction
notes: ISM001-055 for idiopathic pulmonary fibrosis reached Phase II — a milestone for AI-discovered drugs. Also advancing AI-discovered oncology candidates.

name: Absci Corporation
description: Generative AI drug creation platform that designs novel therapeutic antibodies de novo. Zero-shot antibody generation creates functional candidates never seen in nature, validated through integrated wet lab.
approach: Generative AI models trained on proprietary wet-lab data with active learning design-build-test cycles
notes: Public (NASDAQ: ABSI). First to demonstrate zero-shot generative antibody design (2023). Partnership with AstraZeneca for oncology antibodies.

name: Chemify
description: Autonomous chemistry platform that digitizes chemical synthesis. The Chemputer system executes reactions from digital code (XDL language), enabling fully autonomous discovery and manufacture of molecules.
approach: Chemical programming language (XDL) + robotic synthesis + AI retrosynthetic planning for autonomous chemical discovery
notes: Lee Cronin is one of the most cited chemists globally. Chemputer autonomously synthesizes any molecule from a digital recipe. Published landmark papers in Science and Nature.

name: Emerald Cloud Lab
description: Cloud-based remote-access laboratory where scientists write code to execute experiments on robotic equipment. Their Symbolic Lab Language (SLL) enables programmatic, reproducible science.
approach: Cloud-based robotic lab infrastructure with programmable experiment execution via Symbolic Lab Language
notes: Pioneer of programmable cloud labs. Enables any AI system to execute physical experiments via code. Backed by Peter Thiel's Founders Fund and a16z.

name: Arctoris
description: Fully automated drug discovery platform. The Ulysses platform combines robotic experimentation with AI-driven experimental design, enabling autonomous closed-loop drug discovery with no manual lab steps.
approach: AI-driven experimental planning + fully robotic wet lab execution with active learning feedback loops
notes: Oxford University spinout. Ulysses platform runs 24/7 robotic experiments with AI-designed protocols. Focus on oncology and rare diseases.

name: Kebotix
description: Self-driving laboratory for materials and chemical discovery. AI predicts promising molecules/materials, robots synthesize and test them, and results feed back to improve the AI in a closed loop.
approach: Generative molecular design + Bayesian optimization + robotic synthesis in closed-loop experimentation
notes: Alain Aspuru-Guzik (pioneer of self-driving labs, Harvard/Toronto professor) is a scientific advisor. One of the earliest dedicated self-driving lab companies.

name: Orbital Materials
description: AI-first materials company building foundation models for materials science. Their LINUS model generates novel atomic structures optimized for target properties like carbon capture and water purification.
approach: Foundation models for materials trained on large-scale databases; generative models outputting novel atomic structures for specified properties
notes: LINUS model is purpose-built for materials, not adapted from drug discovery. Focus on climate-relevant materials (carbon capture, clean water). Ex-Google DeepMind team.

name: Elicit
description: LLM-powered research workflow automation platform for literature review, data extraction, structured synthesis, and systematic reviews. Spun out of the Ought nonprofit.
approach: LLM-powered research workflow automation with structured data extraction and synthesis
notes: One of the most widely used AI research agents. Spun out of Ought nonprofit. Focuses on systematic reviews and structured evidence synthesis.

name: Consensus
description: AI-powered academic search engine that extracts and synthesizes findings directly from scientific papers, providing evidence-based answers to research questions.
approach: AI-powered evidence extraction and synthesis from scientific literature at scale
notes: Evidence-based scientific search engine. Extracts claims and findings from papers rather than just returning links.

name: Genesis Therapeutics
description: AI drug discovery using physics-informed deep learning for molecular design. GEMS platform achieves high accuracy in modeling molecular interactions for autonomous drug candidate identification.
approach: Neural network potentials for molecular dynamics, physics-informed ML, and free energy perturbation calculations
notes: $150M Series B in 2023 led by a16z bio. Physics-based approach differentiates from purely data-driven competitors. Internal drug pipeline in oncology and inflammation.

name: XtalPi
description: AI + quantum physics platform for drug discovery. Predicts crystal structures, molecular properties, and drug-target interactions using cloud-based quantum mechanical simulations combined with machine learning.
approach: Cloud-based quantum mechanics simulation + AI/ML for crystal structure prediction, molecular property prediction, and drug design
notes: $400M Series D in 2021. One of the most well-funded AI drug discovery companies globally. Partnerships with Pfizer and other major pharma.

name: Strateos
description: Cloud-based robotic laboratory platform enabling remote, automated execution of biology and chemistry experiments. Acquired by Ionis Pharmaceuticals to integrate autonomous lab capabilities into antisense drug discovery.
approach: Cloud-accessible robotic labs with API-driven experiment scheduling and automated protocol execution
notes: Acquired by Ionis Pharmaceuticals. Previously known as Transcriptic. Pioneer of the 'cloud lab' concept — fully remote robotic experiment execution.

name: Atinary Technologies
description: Self-driving laboratory platform using Bayesian optimization and AI to autonomously design, execute, and analyze experiments. Focuses on accelerating materials and chemical discovery through closed-loop automation.
approach: Bayesian optimization and multi-objective AI for autonomous experimental design and closed-loop discovery
notes: ETH Zurich / Cambridge spinout. SDLab platform orchestrates multi-objective Bayesian optimization for self-driving chemistry and materials labs.

name: Generate:Biomedicines
description: Generative AI platform for de novo protein therapeutic design. Uses machine learning to generate novel protein drugs across modalities — antibodies, peptides, enzymes — from scratch rather than optimizing existing molecules.
approach: Generative machine learning models for de novo protein design across therapeutic modalities
notes: Flagship Pioneering portfolio company. Chroma generative model designs proteins with specified structure and function. Multiple programs in preclinical development.

name: Terray Therapeutics
description: AI drug discovery company combining massive-scale combinatorial chemistry (trillions of molecular measurements) with deep learning to map drug-target interactions at unprecedented resolution and discover novel small molecule therapeutics.
approach: Trillion-scale combinatorial chemistry + deep learning for molecular interaction mapping and small molecule drug discovery
notes: $220M Series B led by a16z bio with NVIDIA participation. Proprietary tNOVA platform generates trillions of molecular measurements to train AI models for drug discovery.
